item.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: OpenAI's first hardware device is reported to be a screenless, AI-guided smart speaker that can move. Weird enough for you?

item.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

item.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

item.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.

item.title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem aware of the responsibility it carries — would appear to be more of the same.

item.title: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to build a new AI dating service, Overtone
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Overtone describes itself as "a voice- and audio-forward service, enabled by AI, that provides highly curated introductions."

item.title: Lucid Motors denies report it’s considering bankruptcy
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The company said the "rumors are completely false" after its stock sank more than 50% on a report that it was weighing the option.

item.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

item.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

item.title: DeepSeek reportedly in talks to raise $1.5B, then IPO
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: DeepSeek, the Chinese large language model developer, is said to be preparing for a 2027 IPO debut as it also looks to raise around $1.5 billion in new funds at a $71 billion valuation.

item.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face limits on how much they spend using AI tools.

item.title: Google Images gets a Pinterest-like redesign focused on discovery
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Now, when users navigate to Google Images, they'll see a "For You" gallery of images tailored to their interests and browsing history.

item.title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: New York has become the first state to temporarily halt approval of large data centers, as Gov. Kathy Hochul argues the AI-driven building boom shouldn’t come at the expense of higher electricity costs, water supplies, or local control.

item.title: Iran abused mobile networks’ vulnerabilities to locate US military in the Middle East, report says
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: The Iranian government exploited well-known flaws in cellphone networks to locate and then strike U.S. military personnel in the build-up and beginning of the war.

item.title: I’m de-influencing you from buying the RingConn 3 (even though it’s pretty)
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: The RingConn 3 actually looks like real jewelry, not a wearable -- but its fitness tracking and headache detection features are disappointing.

item.title: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius' compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.

item.title: The real AI race may no longer be at the frontier
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue says enterprises increasingly want open models, due to cost, accessibility, and ownership. Do frontier models still matter if most production AI ends up running on open models?

item.title: Telegram’s shortlink domain is back online after day-long suspension
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Telegram CEO Pavel Durov confirmed an outage in a tweet, saying that shortlinks to the messaging app had "stopped working."

item.title: Spotify expands its AI push with a ChatGPT-like music assistant
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Spotify is rolling out a new AI-powered conversational feature that lets Premium subscribers chat with the app to discover music, podcasts, audiobooks, and more.

item.title: Superhuman’s new auto-draft feature almost makes me like AI replies
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Superhuman’s latest AI email drafting feature is its most convincing yet, generating replies that often required little to no editing in our testing.
